e2e.summary: All 18 responsive E2E tests passed across chromium, firefox, and webkit (6 viewports each: iphone-se, iphone-11, ipad, ipad-land, laptop, desktop). No horizontal overflow, no fatal console errors, hero heading visible across all viewports. Run completed in ~35.6s against production server on port 3200.

recommendations: Reduce unused JavaScript (~110ms savings on desktop)
Eliminate render-blocking resources (~4ms savings, score 0.5)
Add valid source maps for large first-party JavaScript (currently missing)
Reduce total byte weight of network payloads (score 0.5)
Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers (score 0.5)
Eliminate render-blocking resources (~45ms savings on mobile)
Minimize main-thread work (mobile mainthread-work-breakdown failing)
Optimize Largest Contentful Paint element (LCP 2.77s on mobile)
Fix color-contrast issues (accessibility audit failing)
Add valid source maps and reduce total byte weight / legacy JS

recommendations.action: On mobile (375/414) the SVG core fallback orb overlaps the hero 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' role badge. The fallback is full-viewport positioned. Make the orb's SVG smaller on small viewports (e.g., scale down with viewBox + max-w / smaller fixed center), and offset it vertically to the top half so the role-badge text below it has clear space. Suggested: limit orb visual to the top 55vh on viewports <= 480px and reduce orb radius proportionally.

The 'CORE // ONLINE' badge + orbiting orange dot is rendering as part of the SVG fallback or HUD overlay and is fixed to the viewport, causing it to bleed visually into projects/career/about sections during scroll. Wrap that badge in a hero-only ScrollTrigger that hides it when hero is scrolled past, OR position it inside the hero section element instead of fixed.

Contact title 'PING // THE_CORE' (or 'OPENING // THE_CORE') still wraps awkwardly to 'ING // THE_CORE' at 375px. The Phase 5 fix used keep-all + clamp(2rem,...) but the visual at 375px shows breakage still. Solution: split the title into two lines deliberately ('PING //' on line 1, 'THE_CORE' on line 2) on viewports <= 480px using a `<br className="sm:hidden">` or grid layout. Also reduce font size further on smallest viewport (clamp(1.5rem,...)).

On mobile (375/414) the projects section shows the SVG orb dominating and the project card content is not visible in the fold. The card is below the orb in the stack. Either: (a) reduce orb size to ~30vh on mobile so card stays in viewport, OR (b) hide the orb entirely on mobile in projects (it's already in hero — duplication is visual noise). Pick whichever fits the design language better.

Mobile hero role badge 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' is visually overlapped by the orb. Add a dark gradient/glass background to the role badge so it remains readable on top of any background, OR position the orb behind a translucent backdrop layer that slightly fades the orb where text overlaps.

issues: Projects: project card slightly overlaps orb visual (busy composition)
Hero orb canvas overlaps the 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' role badge text
Persistent CORE // ONLINE badge and orbiting orange dot bleed into projects/career/about sections (visual leak from hero canvas)
Contact heading wraps awkwardly to 'ING // THE_CORE'
Projects section shows large orb but project card content not visible at this viewport
Hero orb visual overlaps 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' role badge text
Projects section: orb dominates and project card not visible in fold
Hero role badge slightly overlapped by orb visual

instructions: For each recommendation, identify the concrete file(s) to patch.

Common patches: code-split heavy imports with next/dynamic + ssr:false, defer non-critical JS with requestIdleCallback, swap large images for next/image with sizes/srcset, drop unused npm deps, add `<link rel="preconnect">` for fonts, tune Bloom intensity, adjust DPR cap, reduce Three geometry detail, replace expensive backdrop-filter with semi-opaque bg, hide HUD on small viewports.
After patching, run `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build`.
prompt.role: Senior frontend engineer applying iterative refinements
prompt.task: Apply each recommendation. Smallest viable patch per issue. Verify build after.
